executive_brief: CourseHub API is a structured course management system designed for FastAPI and PostgreSQL. It implements a role-based architecture separating Instructor CRUD operations from Student enrollment and progress tracking. The system utilizes a JWT-based authentication layer, asynchronous database sessions, and an external email integration via Resend for onboarding, all wrapped in a standardized API response envelope.

architectural_constraints: Coverage: Target >= 80% on business logic (routes, services, security)
Data Validation: Enrollment progress must be an integer between 0 and 100 inclusive
Data Validation: Course price_cents must be stored as an integer (cents)
Data Integrity: Unique constraint on Module (course_id, order)
Data Integrity: Unique constraint on Enrollment (student_id, course_id)
Isolation: Instructors can only manage/update courses where instructor_id == current_user.id
Isolation: Students can only access/update enrollments where student_id == current_user.id
Business Rule: Course deletion rejected with 409 Conflict (ACTIVE_ENROLLMENT_CONSTRAINT) if active enrollments exist
Auth: Access token expiration 15 minutes; Refresh token expiration 7 days
Response Pattern: All endpoints must be wrapped in APIResponse envelope (data, meta, errors)

maturity_assessment: The specification is exceptionally detailed, covering everything from directory structure to specific HTTP status codes and async session management. With a health index of 100.0 and no high or medium severity gaps, the project is professionally framed. Only a low-severity absence of an 'Open Questions' section is noted, which does not impede technical execution. Status: READY.
critical_dependencies: RESEND_API_KEY: Mandatory environment variable for email service
PostgreSQL: Async database instance required for migrations and runtime
JWT: Dependency for all protected routes via HTTPBearer
Cascade Deletion: Modules must be deleted automatically upon Course deletion
Referential Integrity: Enrollment strict foreign key dependence on User and Course entities
AsyncSession: Dependency injection required for all service-layer database interactions
